title: Accessible Puzzle Interface and Multichannel Feedback · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Provide a synchronized structured-text grid, route or step list, coordinates, and status that convey essential puzzle state.
Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.
Provide keyboard navigation, remapping, visible focus, large targets, clear labels, no traps, and announced state changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-accessibility-evidence-zkswkp" that uses case 8815 to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-accessibility-evidence-zkswkp
# Competency: maze-accessibility-evidence.
# Case 8815: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_accessibility_evidence_zkswkp(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_zkswkp = "zkswkp-pkwskz-8815"
    assert evidence_variant_zkswkp
    evidence_axes_zkswkp = (
        "gridzkswkp",
        "topology8815",
        "searchpkwskz",
        "eventzkswkp8815",
        "access8815zkswkp",
        "recoverypkwskz8815",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_zkswkp)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Accessibility Evidence structural check is only one gate. keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.
